prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel animal called a wren has a property: it can be activated by one of its colored markings. Your job is to figure out which animal in the perception scenario is the wren-analog, and therefore which colored marking on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three animals in an enclosure: a wren, a vro, and a brall. The wren is-beneath the vro. The vro is-to-the-left-of the brall. The wren has a blue colored marking on top, a yellow colored marking on side, and a purple colored marking on bottom. The vro has a yellow colored marking on top and a blue colored marking on bottom. The brall has a purple colored marking on top and a blue colored marking on side. Touching the blue marking on the wren activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three animals in an enclosure: a bep, a fip, and a clop. The clop is-beneath the fip. The fip is-to-the-left-of the bep. The bep has a purple colored marking on top and a yellow colored marking on bottom. The fip has a yellow colored marking on top and a blue colored marking on side. The clop has a blue colored marking on top, a yellow colored marking on side, and a purple colored marking on bottom.

Which animal in the perception scenario is the wren-analog, and which colored marking activates it?
